steps.title: Clear fractions by multiplying through
steps.content:
Notice that $x^2 - 25 = (x-5)(x+5)$. Multiply both sides of the equation by $(x-5)(x+5)$ to eliminate all denominators:

$$25x = x(x+5) + 20(x-5)$$

steps.title: Expand and simplify
steps.content:
Expand both terms on the right side:

$$25x = x^2 + 5x + 20x - 100$$

Combine like terms:

$$25x = x^2 + 25x - 100$$

Subtract $25x$ from both sides:

$$0 = x^2 - 100$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Rearrange to get $x^2 = 100$. Taking the square root of both sides:

$$x = \pm 10$$

The positive solution is 10.
